column1: flow
puntina della mappa -> suggerimento — type=association; label="n"; explanation="a puntina della mappa is associated with n suggerimenti."
flow 1: a puntina della mappa is associated with suggerimento.
the diagram models a relationship between a "puntina della mappa" (map point) and "suggerimento" (suggestion).
a map point contains geographical and weather data.
a suggestion contains a title, text, image, and a category related to weather conditions.
the association indicates that a map point can be linked to multiple suggestions.

the diagram models a process for retrieving data based on location selection.
the process starts at the initial screen and proceeds linearly through the actions.
the user first selects a state, region, and city, then selects their current location.
a request is then sent to the server, and the returned data is presented.
there are no decision points or alternative flows in this diagram.

the diagram illustrates a process of updating a map.
it starts with an initial map state and proceeds to reload data by scrolling down.
the server then receives the request and sends new data, resulting in an updated map.
the diagram depicts a linear flow without any branching or looping.

flow 1: mappa iniziale -> premere il pulsante per i suggerimenti -> schermata suggerimenti
the diagram depicts a simple workflow for displaying suggestions.

it starts at the "mappa iniziale" (initial screen) and proceeds to the "schermata suggerimenti" (suggestion screen) after the user interacts with "premere il pulsante per i suggerimenti" (press the button for suggestions).
the diagram represents a linear flow with no branching or looping.

the diagram illustrates the flow of data and control within an application for retrieving air quality data.
the user initiates the application, which then triggers a series of requests to an external airvisual api.
the api provides data related to countries, states, regions, and cities.
a button click initiates a specific data retrieval process, and the retrieved data is then presented to the user.
the diagram focuses on the data retrieval and presentation sequence, without detailing error handling or user interaction beyond the initial application start and button click.

flow 2: loadcountries() -> loadstates() -> recupera le regioni() -> recupera le città() -> intent(airqualityactivity.this, mainactivity.class) -> getlocation() -> requestpermission() / access_fine_location / checkselfpermission() / permission_granted
the diagram models the workflow of an air quality application, starting from user interaction and progressing through data retrieval from an external api.
the main flow involves loading countries, states, regions, and cities, followed by retrieving data from the airvisual api and presenting it to the user.
location permission is requested and granted as part of the process.
the diagram highlights the interaction between the user, the main activity, and the external api.
utente -> widget provider — type=message; label="onenable()"; explanation="user enables the widget"
widget provider -> airvisual api — type=message; label="updateappwidget()"; explanation="widget provider requests data from the api"
airvisual api -> widget provider — type=message; label="recupera i dati"; explanation="api returns data to the widget provider"
widget provider -> utente — type=message; label="onupdate()"; explanation="widget provider updates the user"
widget provider -> utente — type=message; label="presenta i dati all'utente"; explanation="widget provider presents data to the user"

flow 1: utente -> widget provider (onenable()) -> widget provider -> airvisual api (updateappwidget()) -> airvisual api -> widget provider (recupera i dati) -> widget provider -> utente (onupdate()) -> widget provider -> utente (presenta i dati all'utente)
the diagram illustrates a sequence of interactions between a user, a widget provider, and an airvisual api.
the user initiates the process by enabling the widget.
the widget provider then requests data from the airvisual api using `updateappwidget()`.
the api responds with data, which the widget provider then uses to update and present the data to the user.
the diagram depicts a single, linear flow of data and control.
utente -> map — type=message; label="l'utente vuole visualizzare la mappa"; explanation="user wants to view the map"
map -> airvisual api — type=message; label="la web view effettua il refresh dei dati"; explanation="web view refreshes the data"
airvisual api -> map — type=message; label="vengono ritornati i nuovi dati"; explanation="new data is returned"
map -> utente — type=message; label="i dati vengono presentati all'utente"; explanation="data is presented to the user"
flow 1: utente -> map -> airvisual api -> map -> utente
the diagram models the process of a user viewing a map and receiving updated data from an airvisual api.
the user initiates the process by wanting to view the map.
the map activity then requests data from the airvisual api.
the api returns the new data to the map activity.
finally, the map activity presents the data to the user.
the diagram depicts a single, linear flow without any branching or looping.
utente -> posizione — type=control_flow; label="l'utente decide la nazione, la regione e la città di interesse"; explanation="user selects location"
posizione -> tips — type=control_flow; label="dalla posizione otteniamo l'aqi della città"; explanation="location provides aqi"
tips -> activitytips — type=control_flow; label="getconsigliperaqi(int aq)"; explanation="calls getconsigliperaqi"
activitytips -> tips — type=control_flow; label="ritorna dati"; explanation="returns data"
tips -> utente — type=control_flow; label="i dati vengono presentati all'utente"; explanation="data is presented to the user"
flow 1: utente -> posizione -> tips -> activitytips -> tips -> utente
the diagram models a process for providing tips based on air quality index (aqi) data.
the user initiates the process by selecting a location.
the system retrieves the aqi for the selected city.
the `getconsigliperaqi` operation is called to obtain tips based on the aqi value.
the tips are then presented to the user.
the diagram shows a linear flow from user input to data presentation.
